title: bromus racemosus var. elongatus
intro: Bromus racemosus var. elongatus, also known as the meadow brome or drooping brome, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Bromus racemosus var. elongatus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, flu, and skin irritations.
